items.title: Opluchting in Den Haag: Sturm Graz vindt elders nieuwe spits
items.summary: Sturm Graz is in de sloturen van de Oostenrijkse transfermarkt aan de haal gegaan met Aston Villa-aanvaller Rory Wilson, die op huurbasis naar het Alpenland trekt. Dat duidt erop dat Sturm geen akkoord kon vinden met ADO Den Haag over een transfer van Luka Reischl.

items.title: Cristiano Ronaldo houdt boycot vol: sterspeler laat opnieuw duel schieten
items.summary: De Cristiano Ronaldo-soap in Saoedi-Arabië krijgt vrijdagavond een nieuw hoofdstuk. De Portugese superster is ontevreden over de gang van zaken in de competitie. Vrijdag zit hij niet bij de wedstrijdselectie van Al-Nassr, dat het opneemt tegen Al-Ittihad.

items.title: Guardiola zwijgt nog over spitsenkeuze op Anfield: 'Haaland is de beste op aarde'
items.summary: Pep Guardiola wil nog niet verklappen wie hij zondag in de spits zet als Manchester City op bezoek gaat bij landskampioen Liverpool. Bij doelpuntenmachine Erling Haaland lijkt er de laatste tijd zowaar wat zand in de motor te zitten, terwijl Omar Marmoush een goed koppeltje vormde met winteraanwinst Antoine Semenyo. De Catalaanse manager hulde zich in nevelen toen hij gevraagd werd wie er op Anfield zou starten. Eén ding wilde hij wel kwijt: Erling Haaland is de beste spits die er is.

items.title: Ronaldo misses 2nd Al Nassr game amid dispute
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo will miss a second straight game for Al Nassr amid his ongoing dispute with the Saudi Pro League.

items.title: Utd's Carrick relishes reunion with Spurs boss
items.summary: Michael Carrick finds himself up against Thomas Frank just five months on from interviewing the Tottenham boss.

items.title: Arteta apologises to Rosenior after 'disrespect'
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has apologised to Chelsea after Liam Rosenior accused the north London side of a "lack of respect" during the warm-ups before Tuesday's Carabao Cup semifinal, second leg.

items.title: Colombia star Rodríguez joins Loons through WC
items.summary: Minnesota United on Friday officially signed Colombia international star forward James Rodríguez through this summer's World Cup.

items.title: Nasser's T20 World Cup predictions: Winners and England's chances
items.summary: Nasser Hussain make his predictions for the 2026 Men's T20 World Cup, which is live in full on Sky Sports from February 7 to March 8.

items.title: Jan Poortvliet, nog altijd bezeten van voetbal
items.summary: Hij was erbij in de WK-finale van 1978 in Argentinië en is bijna 48 jaar later, als zeventigjarige, interim-trainer van FC Eindhoven. Jan Poortvliet is nog even fanatiek als altijd. Hij traint, coacht, kijkt en zit nooit om een mening verlegen.

items.title: Ronaldo misses second Al Nassr game amid ongoing strike
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo has missed a second straight Al Nassr game after not featuring in the Saudi Pro League fixture against Al Ittihad on Friday.

items.title: Bekerloting: kraker in Nijmegen en Noord-Hollandse derby
items.summary: Vrijdag is er geloot voor de halve finale van de KNVB-beker. AZ speelt tegen Telstar, NEC krijgt PSV op bezoek.

items.title: Paul could target Tommy Fury rematch in UK next
items.summary: Jake Paul is to resume his boxing career later this year after surgery on his broken jaw and could target a rematch with Tommy Fury in the UK.

items.title: Carrick has restored hope to Man United, but it's what comes next that matters
items.summary: Michael Carrick's three-game winning streak has certainly lifted the mood at Old Trafford. But building on this early form will truly test Man United's mettle.

items.title: What's happened to Brighton? Palace game crucial for Hurzeler
Harper accuses Dubois of 'disrespect' before tense face-off
items.summary: Caroline Dubois continued to wage psychological war with rival Terri Harper at the launch press conference ahead of their April 5 world title unification, live on Sky Sports.

items.title: Why should I not express how I feel? - Guardiola
items.summary: Pep Guardiola defended his stance of speaking up about global conflicts by asking, "why should I not express what I feel, just because I am a manager?"

items.title: 'Football aspect of Liverpool season not so important after Jota'
items.summary: Former Liverpool goalkeeper Caoimhin Kelleher says performances and results at his old club are not quite as important this season following the death of Diogo Jota.

items.title: ‘I enjoy the Championship’: Burnley fans stay positive as relegation looms
items.summary: Clarets host must-win game with West Ham on Saturday but face a third demotion in three Premier League seasons “I still enjoy the day out, I just don’t let the results affect me any more,” says the Burnley season-ticket holder Mark Bentley. Fifteen games without a Premier League win and 11 points adrift of safety, the Clarets are facing relegation the season after promotion for a second time in two years. Sparring with the best should bring glorious enjoyment at Turf Moor but instead they have three victories from 24 games, beating the two other promoted sides and rock-bottom Wolves, with survival chances looking worse than slim. Relegation rivals West Ham visit on Saturday, and another defeat would remove any faint hope that Burnley could turn it around and leave their record over recent seasons reading: down, up, down, up, down. Beating Sunderland in their first home game brought optimism after a summer where money was spent, notably bringing in the 96-cap Kyle Walker after eight years of success at Manchester City, but that feelgood factor has dwindled. Continue reading...

items.title: Settled Xavi Simons finds his groove at Spurs after time spent in the shadows
items.summary: Dutch midfielder has rubbed shoulders with Messi, Neymar and Mbappé but is now making his own name For Xavi Simons, it felt like a point of no return. He and his Tottenham teammates had nothing more to lose. The FA Cup tie at home to Aston Villa on 10 January was going badly. Played off the park by Unai Emery’s team, they were booed off by their own fans at half-time. They were losing 2-0. Simons takes it personally when things are not going well and that had been the case, pretty much, since his £51.8m move from RB Leipzig last August. The 22-year-old knows his levels. These were not them. Continue reading...
